Author.title: Author
Author.description: Represents an author of blog posts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Author entity.

name.description: The full name of the author.

bio.description: A short biographical description of the author.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the author's profile picture.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification category for blog posts and products.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the category (e.g., 'Tendências', 'Dicas de Estilo').

slug.description: A URL-friendly version of the category name.

description.description: A brief description of the category.

imageUrl.description: URL for an image representing this category, used in featured category sections.

BlogPost.title: BlogPost
BlogPost.description: Represents an article or post on the FashionGirl blog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BlogPost entity.

title.description: The title of the blog post.

slug.description: A URL-friendly version of the blog post title.

excerpt.description: A brief summary or excerpt of the blog post content.

content.description: The full content of the blog post, possibly in HTML or Markdown format.

imageUrl.description: URL to the main image associated with the blog post.

authorId.description: Reference to the Author of this blog post. (Relationship: Author 1:N BlogPost)

categoryIds.description: References to Categories associated with this blog post. (Relationship: Category N:N BlogPost)

publishDate.description: The date and time when the blog post was published.

readTimeMinutes.description: Estimated reading time of the blog post in minutes.

isFeatured.description: Indicates if the blog post should be highlighted in featured sections.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents an item in the FashionGirl product catalog for showcase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

slug.description: A URL-friendly version of the product name.

imageUrl.description: URL to the main image of the product.

linkUrl.description: An external URL to the product's detail page or purchase link.

categoryIds.description: References to Categories associated with this product. (Relationship: Category N:N Product)

HeroCarouselSlide.title: HeroCarouselSlide
HeroCarouselSlide.description: Represents an individual slide in the main homepage hero carousel.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HeroCarouselSlide entity.

title.description: The main title displayed on the carousel slide.

imageUrl.description: URL to the background image for the carousel slide.

altText.description: Alternative text for the slide image, for accessibility.

linkUrl.description: The URL where clicking on the slide should navigate.

order.description: The display order of the slide within the carousel.

Page.title: Page
Page.description: Represents static content pages of the application, such as 'About Us', 'Contact', or 'FAQ'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Page entity.

slug.description: A URL-friendly version of the page title (e.g., 'nossa-historia').

content.description: The full content of the page, typically in HTML or Markdown format.

imageUrl.description: URL to a primary image associated with the page, if any.

imageAltText.description: Alternative text for the page's primary image.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time when the page content was last updated.

definition.description: Contains details for blog post authors. Accessible to all users for public display.

params.name: authorId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Author document.

definition.description: Contains definitions for content and product categories. Accessible to all users for navigation and filtering.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Category document.

definition.description: Stores individual blog articles, including content, images, and metadata. Accessible to all users for public reading.

params.name: blogPostId
params.description: Unique identifier for the BlogPost document.

definition.description: Stores product catalog items for display, with links to external purchase pages. Accessible to all users for browsing.

params.name: productId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Product document.

definition.description: Manages individual slides for the homepage's main hero carousel. Accessible to all users.

params.name: slideId
params.description: Unique identifier for the HeroCarouselSlide document.

definition.description: Holds content for static application pages (e.g., 'Nossa História', 'Contato', 'FAQ'). Accessible to all users for public reading.

params.name: pageId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Page document.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for the FashionGirl Blog is designed to prioritize public readability, scalability, and ease of management, adhering strictly to the core design principles.

**Authorization Independence:** All primary content collections (`/authors`, `/categories`, `/blogPosts`, `/products`, `/heroCarouselSlides`, `/pages`) are designed for public read access. This eliminates the need for complex authorization dependencies (`get()` calls) in security rules for read operations. Since the content is globally accessible to any user (including anonymous), no `request.auth.uid` checks or denormalized authorization fields (like a `members` map or `ownerId` for read access) are required for these collections. This inherently makes read operations atomically independent and simplifies rule debugging. Write access, however, would be restricted to authenticated administrators. For this, a separate `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection would store administrator UIDs, ensuring that write operations on the content collections are authorized independently by checking the existence of `request.auth.uid` in this dedicated role collection, without needing to query parent documents or content-specific authorization fields.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structural segregation mandate ensures that all documents within a given collection share the same security posture – in this case, public readability. This means clients can perform `list` operations (queries) on collections like `/blogPosts` or `/products` without needing security rules to filter results based on user permissions. The rules simply allow all reads (`allow read: if true;`), ensuring that any query will return a complete and authorized set of public data. There is no mixing of public posts with private drafts in the same collection that would necessitate complex filtering logic within the rules. Featured content (e.g., `isFeatured` in `BlogPost`) is handled by application-level queries and not by security rules attempting to restrict document visibility. Categories, authors, and carousel slides are also globally readable, supporting efficient `list` operations for navigation and content display.
